witnesses.description: A rational point and its image under inversion in the unit circle.

checks.claim: Inversion in the unit circle maps (2,1) to (2/5,1/5).
checks.notes: The validator recomputes |(2,1)|^2 = 5 and multiplies the point by 1/5.

checks.claim: The squared distances of a unit-circle inversion point and its image multiply to 1.
The center, point, and inverse image are collinear.
checks.notes: The validator recomputes the determinant of p and p' and checks it is zero.

checks.claim: Inversion in the unit circle maps (2,1) to a point with x-coordinate 1/2.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact inversion replay computes inverse x-coordinate 2/5, while the malformed row claims 1/2; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final equality conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: The squared distances of a unit-circle inversion point and its image multiply to 2.

data.certificate: The resource regression parses the source SMT-LIB artifact, emits UnsatFarkas evidence, and independently checks the certificate.

checks.notes: Exact inversion replay computes |p|^2 * |I(p)|^2 = 1 for the unit-circle row, while the malformed row claims 2; the source QF_LRA artifact isolates that final scalar conflict for Farkas checking.

checks.claim: General Euclidean inversion theorems, circle-line correspondences, and power-of-a-point require future Lean reconstruction.

data.target_theorem_shape: Coordinate-free inversion theorems including circle-line correspondences, angle preservation, power of a point, and generalized circle inversions
data.future_checker: Lean module with no sorryAx dependencies plus exact coordinate certificate bridges for finite inversion witnesses

checks.notes: The finite rows check one fixed rational inversion witness; general inversion geometry remains proof-assistant work.
